text: DIRECTIVE 2011/65/EU OF THE EUROPEAN PARLIAMENT AND OF THE COUNCIL of 8 June 2011 on the restriction of the use of certain hazardous substances in electrical and electronic equipment (RoHS II) including delegated act 2015/863/EU Directive 2011/65/EU (RoHS II) restricts the use of certain hazardous substances in electrical and electronic equipment. Thus electrical and electronic equipment that is in the scope of the directive may not contain Pb, Hg, Cr(VI), PBB and PBDE above 0,1% and Cd above 0,01% in the homogenous material unless an exemption, defined by the directive, is applicable. In the delegated act 2015/863/EU the restricted substances are enlarged by phthalate substances: DEHP, BBP, DBP and DIBP in concentrations above 0,1% in homogenous material. HARTING is aware of the regulations of directive 2011/65/EU and, in particular, of the requirements of customers. Although most HARTING products are not in the scope of 2011/65/EU, nearly all HARTING products comply with the substance requirements of 2011/65/EU. HARTING products don’t contain phthalates DEHP, BBP, DBP or DIBP in concentrations above 0,1% in homogenous materials. February 2025
